entry.title: Setting Up Alerts for SAP Cloud Integration Using SAP Alert Notification Service
entry.content:
Overview:

Goal: Send automatic alerts email when an iFlow fails in SAP Cloud Integration.

Architecture Flow: SAP Cloud Integration → SAP Alert Notification Service → Notification Channel (Email/Teams/Webhook)

In one of my previous blogs, I explained how to transport integration content using SAP Cloud Transport Management Service. You can read it here:

Content Transport Using SAP Cloud Transport Management Service for SAP CI CF Environment

Prerequisites:

  • SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration capability) subscription
  • SAP BTP subaccount
  • SAP Alert Notification service instance created
  • Proper role collections assigned:
    • Integration Developer
    • Alert Notification Administrator

Configuration:

Step 1: Create Alert Notification Service Instance in BTP

Go to Global Account->Subaccount ->Service Marketplace->Create

Create Instance as per below

Create Service key for above instance to use in step 3 for standard iflow configuration

Navigate to instance created above and create service key as per below.

View credential and select form for better view.

You will use above details to create Oauth2 Credential and Maintain URL in step 3. 

Step 2: Create a Notification Channel for ANS Instance in BTP

A . Create Action

Navigate to Instance created in previous step and select instance

Go to Actions->Create as per below

Select Action type as Email

In Email Address field provide email id on which you want to receive alerts.

Action is created now but not confirmed.

B. Activation of Action 

Select action created in previous step 2.A which will show status as Not Confirmed. 

To confirm the status, check your email inbox provided in previous step which has confirmation token. You can either enter confirmation token by Confirm Action button or by selecting link provided in email, refer below.

Email received for activation of action

After confirmation the status of action will change to Enabled. 

C. Adding subscription and Event condition for action

Go to Subscriptions ->Create as per below

Create condition with event type equal to CPIIntegrationFlowExecutionFailure and select the same

Select the action created in Step 2.A and save.

The status of action will show Condition and Action as per below for Subscription.

Step 3: Cloud Integration iflow Configuration

Copy the standard iflow of Alert notification from discovery to Design and configure it as per below.

Package Name : SAP Cloud Integration with SAP Alert Notification service for SAP BTP

Iflow Name : Send notifications for failed Message Processing Logs

For iflow configuration, we need to create Instance and key of Process Integration Runtime service in

BTP Global Account -> Service Marketplace -> Process Integration Runtime Service ->Create

Select plan as api and create

Navigate to instance for Service Key creation 

Once key is created click on view credentials and select form for better view.

We are going to use details from this service key for Alert Iflow Configuration. 

CI Tenant BASE URL will be URL from above Service Key.

Credential will be OAuth2 Client Credential - In this case we have maintained CPI_USER_OAUTH creds in security material, used Client id, client secret and token URL from above key.

Monitor->Security Material ->Create Oauth2 Client Credential->Deploy and use the name of this credential in iflow configuration.

Configure Timer as per your requirement. Whenever this iflow run it will fetch the failed message logs from Message Processing Logs API and will send to Alert Notification Service from that point email alert will be sent to email id maintained in Action under ANS.

In More section - Service Resource Event URL is from Alert Notification Service key from Step 1.

Append /cf/producer/v1/resource-events at the end of ANS url. 

Service Technical Client Name is SAP Alert Notification Service Oauth2 client credential name maintained in security material from the key created in step 1.

Monitor->Security Material ->Create Oauth2 Client Credential->Deploy

Deploy the iflow.

Step 4: Testing

Go to cloud integration create iflow with wrong endpoint and run this to fail so that we can have log for failed message. Post that run standard iflow to send failed message logs to ANS.

Iflow created and deployed as per below to fail in Cloud Integration 

After deployment iflow failed with error message 

Now run the standard alert notification filow to fetch this failure log and to send this to ANS.

You will received alert to email address configured in ANS as per below.  

Summary - 

We have learnt:

How to configure ANS service to receive SAP Cloud integration Iflow failures 

SAP Cloud integration Standard Alert Notification iflow configuration 

Reference:

What is SAP Alert Notification Service for SAP BTP? | SAP Help Portal

If you have any questions, feel free to leave them in the comments below.

entry.title: Data Size of a Business Unit
entry.content:
Dear Expert, 

 

As we plan to export activity data from one of our business units to the AWS database, we would like to find out the current data size of those activities and customer data in GB, TB, etc.

Are there any screens to display this information in UI? Could you please directly provide the size, so the database team will spare that amount?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Data Size of a Business Unit
entry.content:
Dear Team,

 

It's possible to use the activity stats API and do something like select sum(docSize) from [activityName]. Note that it's per activity type you can do the same on stats on profiles/groups.

For example:
https://cdp.eu5.gigya.com/api/businessunits/"Your Business Unit"/views/"Your View"/activities/stats?query=select sum(docSize) from [Clickstream Data-App]
Notes:
  1. This needs to be done for each activity type by name - inside the square brackets
  2. This is an approximation of the size, as it is simply the number of characters in the document before it is saved to our DB
  3. The result is in bytes

Best Regards,

Tamas

entry.title: Reversing Entries in foreign currency result in balance in GC when amount is 0.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are using the periodic translation method and consolidating quarterly.

In scenarios where the subsidiary makes corrections via journal entries (DT14) and then updates their books for the next reporting period (0C) which is reflected in the load, we end up with LC balances =0  but the GC balance displays the periodic translation amount. 

Any way to overcome this?

We are using quarterly consolidation so the journal reverses in period 07 in our example. 
Thought to change the effective date of the 30/9 exchange rate to be as of 31/7/25 so that the periodic reversal will be translated at the new rate.  This will resolve the scenario below, but then, the JE reversals without a correcting 0C balance will be translated at a new rate.  This will result in a GC balance (-1000 x4= 4,000) + (1000 x4.5=4,500) = 500.

Any ideas on how to address?

Another option, maybe cumulative/standard currency translation instead of periodic

Using Group Reporting Public 2602.

Please see example below.

Regards,

Eyal
